reasoning: As of May 14, 2025, Mahmoud Khalil's deportation is blocked by a federal court stay in the District of New Jersey. Although an immigration judge ruled him 'removable' on April 11, this process is subject to a BIA appeal. More critically, U.S. District Judge Michael Farbiarz is presiding over a separate habeas and constitutional challenge; his April 30 ruling (retaining jurisdiction) and May 7 order (demanding detailed legal grounds from the government) indicate the litigation is deep in the merits stage. With the Third Circuit's May 6 rejection of the government's attempt to move the case, the stay in New Jersey remains robust. Given the complexity of the First Amendment and 'foreign policy' issues involved, it is extremely unlikely that all legal barriers will be cleared and the physical deportation of a legal permanent resident carried out within the remaining 6.5 weeks before July 1, 2025.
